steps.title: Find the base area
steps.content: Total surface area equals base area plus lateral surface area. Subtract: $$70,560 = \text{base} + 38,160$$ $$\text{base} = 32,400 \text{ square inches}$$

steps.title: Find the base side length
steps.content: The base is a square with area 32,400. For a square: $$s^2 = 32,400$$ $$s = 180 \text{ inches}$$

steps.title: Find the slant height
steps.content: For a square pyramid, lateral surface area equals $2sl$, where $l$ is the slant height: $$38,160 = 2(180)l$$ $$38,160 = 360l$$ $$l = 106 \text{ inches}$$

steps.title: Apply Pythagorean theorem to find pyramid height
steps.content: In a right square pyramid, the slant height $l$, height $h$, and distance from center to edge midpoint form a right triangle. This distance is $s/2 = 90$: $$l^2 = h^2 + 90^2$$ $$106^2 = h^2 + 90^2$$ $$11,236 = h^2 + 8,100$$ $$h^2 = 3,136$$ $$h = 56 \text{ inches}$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.
